conflicts.description: The previous sheet compressed the journey into exact day counts and a 5-6 week span, but the manuscript's repeated Sable markers show the pursuit is already six weeks old by the chapter 11 intercut and continues after that.
conflicts.canonical_value: Estimated span revised to approximately six to eight weeks, with key-event timing loosened to relative markers instead of brittle day-by-day counts.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript gives repeated explicit elapsed-time cues later in the book; those are more causally embedded than the old sheet's compressed day numbers, so the timeline was broadened rather than forced back into the earlier estimate.

conflicts.description: The old sheet gave a 4-5 day stretch from Thessa's settlement to the Margins border, but the manuscript reaches the broken bridge and boundary river three days after leaving Thessa.
conflicts.canonical_value: Detailed distance strings were removed from the continuity sheet; the broken-bridge river crossing occurs three days south of Thessa's settlement and finer route timing stays in outline/spatial_layout.json.
conflicts.resolution_note: Rule 11 makes the spatial layout authoritative for route detail. The manuscript gives a concrete three-day marker, so the old distance block was treated as an over-specific sheet error rather than new canon.

conflicts.description: The old sheet described Maren as a leader in the sub-Ledger, but the manuscript only gives a specific civic role: she is on the southern district committee handling infrastructure and intake.
conflicts.canonical_value: Maren is a former route explorer who now serves on the southern district committee for infrastructure and intake.
conflicts.resolution_note: The manuscript names a concrete committee job and does not explicitly support the broader 'leader' label, so the sheet was narrowed to the directly stated role.

conflicts.description: The old sheet stated that Fallow knows vanished zeros are 'elsewhere,' but the manuscript only supports witness knowledge that people disappear at the Scour and leave no body behind.
conflicts.canonical_value: Fallow knows that people who hit zero at the Scour disappear; Fallow has personally seen Sera and Hoster vanish, but Fallow does not know the destination.
conflicts.resolution_note: Canonical stability favored narrowing the claim to what Fallow explicitly witnesses rather than preserving an inference the manuscript does not put in Fallow's mouth.

conflicts.description: The old sheet treated Sable's protective false report as a chapter 19 event only, but the manuscript shows she had already filed a false natural-depletion mission-complete report by chapter 15 and then reuses the lie after the crossing.
conflicts.canonical_value: Sable's official cover story starts by chapter 15 with a false natural-depletion report and is reaffirmed after chapter 18 as 'level-zero termination, no threshold event observed.'
conflicts.resolution_note: The earlier lie materially changes what the Hoarded believe during the final approach, so the sheet now records both concealment beats instead of only the final one.
